data.title: AI-Generated Persona Used in Crypto Scam Network
data.description: Blockchain sleuth ZachXBT posted on X, highlighting concerns about an AI-generated persona, Dr. Jihoon Park, purportedly with an IQ of 312, used to promote crypto scams. The persona, associated with the account @Jihooncrypto, is part of a network pushing fake news through multiple accounts. The revelation raises questions about the susceptibility of individuals, particularly older generations, to AI-driven misinformation in the cryptocurrency space.

data.title: Tether has hired KPMG to audit USDT.
data.description: According to Tech in Asia, Tether, the issuer of the stablecoin USDT, has selected KPMG as its auditor and hired PwC to assist in improving its internal systems. This move is the clearest signal yet that Tether is moving towards a comprehensive financial statement audit, which will go beyond the current monthly proof of reserves issued by BDO Italia and comprehensively examine its assets, liabilities, controls, and reporting processes.

data.title: Nansen Expands Integration Options for AI Agents
data.description: Nansen, a blockchain data platform, has announced new integration capabilities for its Nansen Agent. According to Foresight News, the platform now supports API endpoints, CLI commands, and Skill access methods, enabling users to directly transfer comprehensive on-chain smart data to their AI agents.

data.title: Bitcoin Transfer to Cumberland DRW Observed
data.description: A transfer of 70 Bitcoin, valued at approximately $4.81 million, was made from an anonymous address to Cumberland DRW at 13:20. According to ChainCatcher, the transaction involved an address starting with bc1q8q62j.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Discusses Timeline for Artificial General Intelligence
data.description:
PANews posted on X (formerly Twitter). In a public debate with e/acc representative Beff Jezos, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin expressed his views on the timeline for the arrival of Artificial General Intelligence (AGI). Buterin stated that while he is not opposed to AGI, he believes that an eight-year timeline is safer than four years.

Buterin emphasized that paths which genuinely consider human involvement and respect individual circumstances cannot be accelerated through sheer computational power. He highlighted the disparity in development, noting that while some are creating advanced technologies, others in the same city struggle with homelessness, a situation he described as an "extremely imbalanced acceleration" characteristic of American society.

He argued that delaying AGI by four years is worthwhile, but a delay of four hundred years is unacceptable. However, he questioned whether humanity truly has the ability to choose an eight-year path for AGI development.

data.title: WINkLink Completes Comprehensive Upgrade of Price Service Page
data.description: WINkLink has officially completed a comprehensive upgrade of its price service page. According to ChainCatcher, the update introduces a new 'Market Statistics' section, which visualizes key market indicators such as market capitalization rankings, circulating and total supply, and 24-hour and 7-day price ranges. Additionally, the asset display method has been optimized to clearly label asset names and symbols, effectively preventing symbol confusion. The deviation threshold and heartbeat parameters can now be viewed directly on the list page, allowing developers and users to access core data more efficiently. As a provider of oracle services within the Tron ecosystem, WINkLink remains committed to delivering transparent and reliable data support.

data.title: Planet Midday News
data.description: 1. Coinbase's policy chief calls for reform of crypto tax rules, saying the current system is outdated; 2. Ethereum developers are not prioritizing framework transaction proposals, Hegota upgrades focus on censorship resistance; 3. US Democratic lawmakers send a letter to the Kansas City Fed, requesting details on approving Kraken's access to the Fed's services; 4. Early ETH ICO participants begin reducing their holdings, selling approximately $19.72 million worth of ETH; 5. Polymarket lists a new question: "When will the Trump administration terminate the criminal investigation into Powell?", with a 56% probability of termination before June; 6. A new address withdraws 340 BTC from Binance, worth approximately $23.14 million; 7. Former Governor of the People's Bank of China: Cryptocurrency and blockchain technology can be discussed, the key is to adapt to user needs; 8. The US has not yet decided whether to appoint a new head of crypto affairs.

data.title: MetaMask Expands Support to HyperEVM Network
data.description: MetaMask announced on March 27 that it now fully supports the HyperEVM network. According to BlockBeats, users must manually add the HyperEVM custom network within MetaMask to access related features. Once added, users can perform cross-chain token exchanges within MetaMask using the HyperEVM network.

data.title: Wildcard's Token Launch Faces Community Skepticism Amid Market Challenges
data.description:
On March 27, Wildcard, a blockchain gaming project previously backed by a $46 million investment led by Paradigm, launched its token generation event (TGE) on Arbitrum. According to BlockBeats, the token initially reached a market cap of $1.1 million but has since decreased to $809,000, with a liquidity pool of only $209,000. The community has expressed concerns about the project's responsibility and potential 'soft rug' tactics.

Wildcard was founded by Paul Bettner and Katy Drake Bettner, who have notable backgrounds in game development, having worked on popular titles such as 'Words With Friends' and 'Lucky's Tale.' In June 2022, Wildcard completed a $46 million Series A funding round led by Paradigm, with participation from Griffin Gaming Partners and Polygon. The project announced the formation of The Wildcard Alliance, focusing on developing the Web3 game 'Wildcard,' a PVP game combining card, MOBA, and competitive elements, initially based on the Polygon chain and later expanding to Arbitrum.

Despite multiple changes to its roadmap, the project's progress has not met expectations. However, in June 2025, Wildcard completed another funding round, securing $9 million led by Arbitrum Gaming Ventures and Paradigm. The funds were intended to accelerate the development of the Wildcard game, the Thousands protocol, and the Thousands.tv platform, which is a creator-driven user acquisition and live streaming system.

The cooling of the crypto market has led to diminished interest in the game and platform, impacting the project's ability to attract funding. Consequently, the token generation event did not receive significant attention from major centralized exchanges (CEX), further contributing to the community's skepticism.

data.title: Spot silver's intraday gains widened to 3%.
data.description: Gate data shows that spot silver has extended its intraday gains to 3%, currently trading at $70.17 per ounce. (Golden10)

data.title: Maxine Waters Seeks Clarification on Kraken's Federal Reserve Account Approval
data.description: Maxine Waters has requested Kansas City Federal Reserve President Jeff Schmid to provide an explanation regarding Kraken's approval for a limited-purpose account and its access to Federal Reserve payment services. According to NS3.AI, Waters is seeking detailed information on Kraken's access to FedACH, FedCash, and Fedwire securities services, as well as the account's restrictions and risk management requirements. She has asked for a written response by April 10, 2026, expressing concerns that the account type may not have a clear basis in current regulations and account access guidelines.

data.title: Megapot Secures $5 Million to Enhance Global Expansion and Platform Upgrades
data.description: Megapot has successfully raised $5 million in a funding round led by Dragonfly Capital. According to NS3.AI, the company plans to utilize the funds to broaden its reach into additional countries and enhance its platform systems. This strategic move aims to strengthen Megapot's position in the competitive market by improving its technological infrastructure and expanding its international presence.

data.title: S&P 500 Index Shows Signs of Peaking, Analysts Advise Caution
data.description: The S&P 500 Index has experienced fluctuating declines, indicating a potential peak and subsequent downturn. According to Ming Pao, technical analysis suggests that the index has reached a peak and is now in a downward trend. Investors are advised to adopt a strategy of selling at high points, as future movements are expected to be rebounds rather than upward trends. Further technical analysis and investment strategies are available in the video content.

data.title: Polaris to Cease Operations in April 2026
data.description: Polaris, an on-chain trading platform, has announced its decision to cease operations on April 17, 2026. According to Foresight News, users are advised to complete all pending transactions and withdraw any remaining assets through the recovery page before this date. The announcement did not specify the reasons behind the closure.

data.title: UK Bill on Assisted Dying Faces Likely Defeat Despite Public Support
data.description: A UK bill aimed at legalizing assisted dying is anticipated to be unsuccessful, despite significant public backing for the measure. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the complexities and controversies surrounding the issue. The bill's failure is seen as a probable outcome, reflecting the ongoing debate and differing perspectives on assisted dying within the UK.

data.title: UAE and Bahrain Collaborate on UN Security Council Resolution
data.description: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) is working in collaboration with Bahrain to advance a resolution at the United Nations Security Council, according to Jin10. This initiative aims to authorize a future special task force. The resolution seeks to enhance cooperation and address pressing international issues through coordinated efforts. The collaboration between the UAE and Bahrain highlights their commitment to fostering global peace and security. Further details on the resolution's objectives and the specific focus of the task force are yet to be disclosed.

data.title: Societe Generale Cancels Backup Offices in France and UK
data.description: Societe Generale has decided to eliminate its backup office locations in France and the UK, according to the Financial Times. This decision follows the bank's move to allow investment bankers to work from home if their primary trading facilities become inoperable. According to Jin10, the bank's strategy reflects a shift in operational planning, prioritizing remote work capabilities over maintaining physical backup sites. This change is part of a broader trend in the financial industry, where institutions are increasingly adopting flexible work arrangements in response to evolving workplace dynamics.

data.title: Britain Faces Challenges in Sustaining Steel Industry
data.description: Britain's attempts to maintain its steel industry are leading to difficult decisions regarding subsidies and tariffs. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the complexities involved in balancing economic support with trade regulations. The government is navigating these challenges as it seeks to preserve jobs and industrial capacity in a competitive global market. The situation underscores the broader economic pressures facing traditional manufacturing sectors in the UK.

data.title: XRP Declines 2.7% Amid Late Sell-Off and Increased Volume
data.description: XRP experienced a 2.7% decrease over the past 24 hours, falling below $1.40 and stabilizing around $1.35 following a significant late sell-off. According to NS3.AI, the trading volume surged during the final-hour decline, indicating that the drop was driven by liquidation-related selling.

data.title: London Faces Housing Shortage Amid Population Growth
data.description: London's population has reached approximately nine million, yet home construction has significantly slowed. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the city's struggle to meet housing demands as its population continues to expand. The stagnation in construction is raising concerns about the availability of affordable housing options for residents. Experts suggest that policy changes and increased investment in housing infrastructure are necessary to address the growing needs of the city's inhabitants. The situation underscores the challenges faced by urban areas experiencing rapid population growth without corresponding development in housing.
